reason: cited_authority malformed (bad kind or ref shape)

reason: The named authority is a status-marker convention, but neither the SKILL text nor a corpus exemplar of the exact marker form is supplied, and the referent already carries 'forthcoming' plus a full descriptive gloss in both §5.3 and §7; the finding itself concedes the referent resolves and is 'live-but-unenforceable rather than dangling.' reason: The named authorities do not uniquely determine the ADR-003 edit. SDD-001 §4.2/§2.2 place predicate evaluation *semantics* as owned by the constraint-validator SDD (itself forthcoming), while DDR-003 §9 routes only condition predicate *vocabulary/grammar* onward; neither settles whether ADR-003's §2.4 phrase 'predicate-evaluation semantics are routed to the forthcoming constraint-validator service design (per DDR-002 §3's obligation-closure assignment)' should be re-cited, re-scoped to vocabulary, or left. Conforming would require choosing the corrected wording and citation among multiple defensible options — an unratified drafting choice.

authority_conformed: author-decision-record-SKILL, Body integrity — 'Tense is earned by enforcement' (a property whose enforcing mechanism is forthcoming is stated in the conditional — 'holds once Condition N lands')
body-integrity 'cold read' rule: tie identifier at first use; canonical 'ai-gateway-service' already carried in ADR-003 §7

existing_claim: The prohibition on any mechanism 'whose function depends on preventing' agent-to-agent communication is a decision this ADR does not surface in its Decision statement (§2, which commits agents that 'collaborate on any route') and rides only in prose. It is stated as absolute — 'no architectural mechanism may exist' — and as a foreclosing commitment on all future designs. The deliberation record (Item 2, self-review LAA note) itself flags this exact clause as a 'conformance-shaped operationalization' rather than the ratified never-preclude commitment, which the ratified text was 'agent-to-agent communication is first-class and never precluded.' The absolute never-a-preventing-mechanism form is a broader smuggled commitment than the ratified never-preclude; §5.2 then re-states it as a hard constraint on all designs.
incoming_claim: §2 commits agents that 'collaborate on any route' and §2.2 states 'agent-to-agent communication is a first-class platform capability: no platform design may preclude it, and no architectural mechanism may exist whose function depends on preventing it.' The second clause is an absolute foreclosing commitment on all future designs, re-stated as a hard constraint in §5.2 ('no design may ... introduce a mechanism whose function depends on preventing it'). The deliberation record's ratified commitment is narrower — 'agent-to-agent communication is first-class and never precluded' — and the session's own self-review flags §2.2's 'no mechanism whose function depends on preventing it' as a 'conformance-shaped operationalization' of the ratified never-preclude commitment, not the ratified commitment itself. The absolute never-a-preventing-mechanism form is a broader smuggled commitment than the ratified never-preclude, and it is never surfaced as its own decision in the §2 statement.

existing_claim: §2.4's enforcement staging (§5.3 risk: 'The Standards plane has no ingestion path until the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record and its port land') depends on a named artifact — 'The forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record' — that is declared load-bearing for the constraint-binding commitment's enforceability but is not present in the artifact set and is cited only as forthcoming. The record's central new commitment (§2.4 constraints-from-graph) rests its enforcement on this unauthored dependency; the dependency is named as a bare cross-reference with no locus, ticket-home, or status marker beyond 'forthcoming,' leaving the consequence (when/whether §2.4 becomes enforceable) undeclared at the altitude a consequence should be declared.
incoming_claim: §2.4's enforcement staging and §5.3's mitigation ('The Standards plane has no ingestion path until the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record and its port land') declare the constraint-binding commitment's enforceability to depend on 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record,' cross-referenced in §7 as a bare forthcoming artifact absent from the set. The referent resolves against ADR-008 §7 ('The forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record — owns the ingestion mechanics realizing the authoritative-source ingestion control'), so it is not dangling; but at its §5.3 and §7 uses it carries no status marker (forthcoming/unauthored) tying it to a resolution locus, leaving the consequence — when/whether §2.4 becomes enforceable — declared only in §5.3 and not marked at its §7 cross-reference.

authority_conformed: DDR-003 §9 (routed item scoped to 'Condition predicate vocabulary — the predicate grammar for scope conditions')
DDR-003 §9 (Condition predicate vocabulary routed onward) vs ADR-003 §7

DDR-002 §3 (obligation-closure/rule_definition→constraint-validator SDD) + SDD-001 §4.2 (constraint-validator as predicate-evaluation single-source)

reason: The named authority (body-integrity: 'an amendment that adds a ruling updates the Decision enumeration') establishes that the Decision block should surface the never-preclude ruling, but it does not uniquely determine the conforming wording of a new sub-decision entry — where it sits, how it is phrased, and whether the fix is instead to soften §2.2/§5.2 all remain unratified choices. Deliberation Item 2 ratifies the commitment's substance but not its Decision-block surfacing form.
authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL, body-integrity: 'An amendment that adds a ruling updates the Decision enumeration' (Decision block is the contract's index); deliberation Item 2 ratifies the never-preclude commitment

reason: The status-marker discipline is already satisfied at the §7 definition point ('The forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record (unauthored)') and the subject is named ('the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record'); the finding's own body concedes §5.3's earned tense 'is proportionate.' The authority names a discipline (subject-named + status-marker) but does not uniquely determine what edit, if any, the §5.3 in-body use point requires — whether to add a parenthetical status marker, restate the earned tense, or leave it — leaving multiple conforming options and no ratified choice among them.

The SKILL body-integrity discipline requires a status marker and named resolution locus, but the ADR-003 §7 entry already carries the status marker '(unauthored)'. What remains — restating the enforcement-gating consequence and/or supplying a named resolution locus (ticket-home / definition-of-done pointer) — is not uniquely determined: the authority does not fix which locus text to use, and ADR-008's parallel entry ('owns the ingestion mechanics realizing the authoritative-source ingestion control (§2.2)') supplies a role clause, not a resolution-locus pointer, so no single conforming edit is entailed.

existing_claim: §2.6 fixes the class of a named component — 'the model-access gateway (the forthcoming ai-gateway-service, §7) is of this class (infrastructure facade — model access, no judgment of record)' — and the metadata Description plus §5.1 name it as a design this record unblocks ('the orchestration engine, solutioning agent, and model-access gateway designs'). This is a component-existence-and-classification decision riding in taxonomy prose. §2.6's own rule states 'This record enumerates no agent roster ... Components are named above only where existing canon already names them.' No cited authority in the set names a model-access gateway or ai-gateway-service: SDD-001 §2.2 states 'LLM access and prompt assembly — no model client exists in this service' and names no downstream gateway; ADR-001 §2.3 commits LLM-as-renderer with no gateway component. The record names a not-yet-canonical component while claiming to name only canon-named ones.
incoming_claim: §2.6 fixes the class of a named component — 'the model-access gateway (the forthcoming ai-gateway-service, §7) is of this class (infrastructure facade — model access, no judgment of record)' — and §5.1 lists 'the model-access gateway design' among the designs this record unblocks, and the metadata Description names 'the model-access gateway' as an unblocked design. This is a component-existence-and-classification decision riding in taxonomy prose. §2.6's own rule immediately follows: 'This record enumerates no agent roster ... Components are named above only where existing canon already names them.' No cited authority in the set establishes a model-access gateway / ai-gateway-service: SDD-001 §2.2 states 'LLM access and prompt assembly — no model client exists in this service' and names no downstream gateway; ADR-001 §2.3 commits LLM-as-renderer without naming a gateway component; §7 lists 'ai-gateway-service' only as forthcoming. The record names a not-yet-canonical component while claiming to name only canon-named ones — a scope defect against its own stated rule, and a smuggled component-existence commitment.

existing_claim: The prohibition on any mechanism 'whose function depends on preventing' agent-to-agent communication is a decision this ADR does not surface in its Decision statement (§2, which commits agents that 'collaborate on any route') and rides only in prose. It is stated as absolute — 'no architectural mechanism may exist' — and as a foreclosing commitment on all future designs. The deliberation record (Item 2, self-review LAA note) itself flags this exact clause as a 'conformance-shaped operationalization' rather than the ratified never-preclude commitment, which the ratified text was 'agent-to-agent communication is first-class and never precluded.' The absolute never-a-preventing-mechanism form is a broader smuggled commitment than the ratified never-preclude; §5.2 then re-states it as a hard constraint on all designs.
incoming_claim: §2.2 commits, in prose, an absolute foreclosure — 'no architectural mechanism may exist whose function depends on preventing it' — and §5.2 restates it as a hard constraint on all future designs ('or introduce a mechanism whose function depends on preventing it'). This is a strictly broader, harder-to-reverse commitment than the never-preclude posture the §2 Decision statement carries (agents that 'collaborate on any route'). An absolute never-a-preventing-mechanism rule forecloses design space across every downstream SDD/DDR for the platform's life, and rides only in §2.2/§5.2 prose — it is not surfaced as its own sub-decision in the §2 enumeration. The deliberation record's own self-review (Leg 3) flags §2.2's 'no mechanism whose function depends on preventing it' as a 'conformance-shaped operationalization' of the ratified never-preclude commitment, whose ratified text was narrower ('agent-to-agent communication is first-class and never precluded'). A foreclosing commitment of this reversibility cost, riding as an operationalizing gloss rather than a first-class Decision, is a scope defect: the Decision block is the contract's index, and this ruling is invisible to a reader who treats the enumeration as complete.

The record's central new commitment (§2.4 constraints-from-graph) rests its enforcement on this unauthored dependency; the dependency is named as a bare cross-reference with no locus, ticket-home, or status marker beyond 'forthcoming,' leaving the consequence (when/whether §2.4 becomes enforceable) undeclared at the altitude a consequence should be declared.
incoming_claim: §2.4's committed constraint-binding pattern rests its enforceability on 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record' (§5.3: 'The Standards plane has no ingestion path until the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record and its port land'). §5.3 states this in earned tense. The §7 cross-reference entry — 'The forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record (unauthored) — the Standards-plane entry path on which §2.4's enforcement stages depend' — carries an unauthored marker and the referent resolves within the set (ADR-008 §7 names the same forthcoming record). The residue is that the §7 entry does not carry a resolution locus (ticket-home / definition-of-done pointer) for the load-bearing dependency on which the record's central new commitment rests, whereas the corpus convention carries navigability toward such referents; a consequence of this magnitude (when/whether §2.4 becomes enforceable) is declared at §5.3 but its resolution path is not tied at the §7 point of introduction.

existing_claim: Attacked the record for claim-fidelity between its Decision statement and body: whether the single-sentence §2 decision and its sub-decisions decide exactly what the ratified deliberation authorized — no smuggled commitments, no ratified commitments dropped. Traced each of the eight ratified items (orchestration model, run anatomy incl. isolation and honest-halt, constraints-from-graph, four-class taxonomy with recursion, non-ruling boundary, RBT-11 fold as Alternative C, interleave/trajectory-through-upstream). The body decides each ratified item at matching altitude, and the RBT-11 fold correctly lands as a rejected alternative (§4.3) rather than a smuggled standalone decision; the trajectory clause correctly advances 'through' upstream amendment (§2.7) rather than pre-empting ADR-002 §2.6. The attack held except for the two §2.2/§2.6 scope defects reported separately.
incoming_claim: Attacked the record for claim-fidelity between its §2 Decision statement and body against the eight ratified deliberation items — hunting smuggled commitments and dropped ratifications. Each ratified item is decided at matching altitude: the orchestration model (§2.1–§2.3), run anatomy including expressible isolation and the honest-halt posture (§2.5), constraints-from-graph (§2.4), the four-class taxonomy with recursion (§2.6), the non-ruling boundary (§2.9), and the RBT-11 directives-bridge fold correctly landed as a rejected alternative (§4.3) rather than a smuggled standalone decision. The trajectory clause (§2.7) advances 'through' upstream amendment rather than pre-empting ADR-002 §2.6. The attack held except for the §2.2, §2.6-model-gateway, and recursion scope defects reported separately.

existing_claim: Attacked §2.7 for quietly widening or retracting ADR-002 §2.6's write-authority assignments (AOE owns run/ReasoningSession lifecycle; the author/executor/authorizer principle) via the coordination trajectory. The record does not: §2.7 explicitly advances 'through the upstream authority structure, never around it,' names run-lifecycle-authorship widening beyond the AOE as a future item requiring the owning record's own amendment, and §2.9 disclaims re-authoring ADR-002 §2.5/§2.6 ('gates in runs instantiate those controls and never re-author them'). §2.6 retains the orchestrator as a distinct class rather than collapsing it into 'just another agent,' preserving ADR-002 §2.6 lifecycle clarity. The consequence and deferral are declared; no unsupported retraction of upstream authority was found at the trajectory clause.
incoming_claim: Attacked §2.7 for quietly widening or retracting ADR-002 §2.6's write-authority assignments (AOE owns run/ReasoningSession lifecycle; the author/executor/authorizer principle) via the coordination trajectory. The consequence and deferral are declared; no unsupported retraction of upstream authority was found at the trajectory clause (the recursion clause's premature commitment is a distinct defect reported separately).

existing_claim: §2.3 asserts, as a load-bearing invariant, that checkpoint gates 'anchor to checkpoints in the run and the graph, not to positions in a call graph; no routing choice can bypass a gate.' §2.2 makes direct agent-to-agent communication first-class ('Direct interaction between agents is permitted on the same terms as orchestrator-mediated interaction') and §2.8 commits that 'opening a direct agent-to-agent route requires no ratification act; the §2.3 invariants are the entire control.' The record supplies no mechanism internal to itself by which a gate anchored to a graph checkpoint binds a direct interaction that touches no graph checkpoint. The ADR-008 controls it cites for gates are, per ADR-008 §6, 'enforced at three-hat review at design time' with runtime instances gateway-mediated — an interaction performing no graph write reaches neither. The universal claim 'no routing choice can bypass a gate' is stated but not substantiated for the direct-route, no-graph-touch interaction the same record makes first-class: a gate anchored to graph checkpoints has nothing to anchor to on such a route.
incoming_claim: §2.3 asserts as a load-bearing invariant that checkpoint gates 'anchor to checkpoints in the run and the graph, not to positions in a call graph; no routing choice can bypass a gate.' §2.2 makes direct agent-to-agent communication first-class ('Direct interaction between agents is permitted on the same terms as orchestrator-mediated interaction'), and §2.8 commits that 'opening a direct agent-to-agent route requires no ratification act; the §2.3 invariants are the entire control.' The unbypassability guarantee is graph-anchored, yet the record supplies no mechanism — internal or cited — by which a gate anchored to a graph checkpoint binds a direct agent-to-agent interaction that touches no graph checkpoint. The ADR-008 controls cited for gates are, per ADR-008 §6, enforced at three-hat review at design time with runtime instances gateway-mediated; an interaction performing no graph write reaches neither the sole-owner gateway (ADR-002 §2.5) nor a graph-anchored gate. The universal claim 'no routing choice can bypass a gate' is stated but unsubstantiated for exactly the direct-route, no-graph-write interaction the same record makes first-class.

existing_claim: Checked each canon-named component instance in §2.6 against its establishing authority in scope. ASA classed as a specialized (reasoning-bearing) agent: conforms to ADR-002 §2.6 ('the Architecture Solutioning Agent (ASA) ... is the authorized author of ReasoningProgress') and DDR-002 §4 (ASA authors ReasoningProgress). knowledge-service classed as the canonical substrate service (executes/enforces without authoring): conforms to ADR-002 §2.5 (sole-owner gateway), §2.6 (gateway is 'never itself the authoring authority'), and SDD-001 §1 ('sole executor of every authoritative write and the author of none'). AOE classed as the orchestrator owning run lifecycle: conforms to ADR-002 §2.6 ('AOE ... owns the ReasoningSession lifecycle only') and SDD-001 §3.4.1. Each named instance's class assignment resolves against its cited authority; the taxonomy names only components existing canon establishes, as §2.6 claims. The attack that a named instance was misclassed or unestablished failed for all three (the model-access gateway instance is faulted separately).
incoming_claim: Attacked each canon-named component instance in §2.6 for misclassification or non-establishment against its cited authority. ASA as a specialized (reasoning-bearing) agent conforms to ADR-002 §2.6 ('the Architecture Solutioning Agent (ASA) ... is the authorized author of ReasoningProgress') and DDR-002 §4 (ASA authors ReasoningProgress). knowledge-service as the canonical substrate service executing without authoring conforms to ADR-002 §2.5 (sole-owner gateway), §2.6 (gateway 'never itself the authoring authority'), and SDD-001 §1 ('sole executor of every authoritative write and the author of none'). AOE as the orchestrator owning run lifecycle conforms to ADR-002 §2.6 ('AOE ... owns the ReasoningSession lifecycle only') and SDD-001 §3.4.1. Each named instance's class assignment resolves against its cited authority; the attack failed for all three (the model-access gateway instance is faulted separately).

existing_claim: Attacked §4.3's rejection rationale for whether the directive-bridge fold is substantiated by standing canon or asserted. §4.3 claims the constraint authority relocates such that 'there is no envelope to protect because there is no envelope,' resting on §2.4's four mechanisms: graph-resident constraints (the Standards plane, DDR-001), gateway consultation (ADR-002 §2.5 sole-owner gateway), version-pinning at consultation (DDR-001's version-pinning model), and evidence linkage in the RG (ADR-001 §2.2). Each cited relocation mechanism exists and is cited correctly in the set — the sole-owner gateway, the version-pinning temporal model, and the capture invariant are all standing accepted canon. The rejection's core — that transport-resident authority is what the bridge's integrity machinery compensated for, and that authority no longer resides in transport — is carried by those upstream mechanisms rather than by argument alone; the residues route to named owning loci (correlation → orchestration/observability; rendering → per-agent designs under ADR-001 §2.3). The attack that the fold left an unhomed integrity concern failed.
incoming_claim: Attacked §4.3's rejection rationale for whether the directive-bridge fold is substantiated by standing canon or asserted — specifically whether the claim 'there is no envelope to protect because there is no envelope' leaves an unhomed integrity concern. §4.3 rests on §2.4's four mechanisms, each verified in-set: graph-resident constraints (the Standards plane, DDR-001), gateway consultation (ADR-002 §2.5 sole-owner gateway), version-pinning at consultation (DDR-001 version-pinning/temporal model), and evidence linkage in the RG (ADR-001 §2.2). Each cited relocation mechanism is standing accepted canon, so the rejection's core — that transport-resident authority is what the bridge's integrity machinery compensated for, and authority no longer resides in transport — is carried by upstream mechanisms rather than by argument alone; the residues route to named owning loci. The attack that the fold left an unhomed integrity concern failed.

existing_claim: §2.2 commits, in prose, an absolute foreclosure — 'no architectural mechanism may exist whose function depends on preventing it' — and §5.2 restates it as a hard constraint on all future designs ('or introduce a mechanism whose function depends on preventing it'). This is a broader, harder-to-reverse commitment than the never-preclude posture the §2 Decision statement carries (agents that 'collaborate on any route'). An absolute never-a-preventing-mechanism rule forecloses design space across every downstream SDD/DDR for the platform's life and is not surfaced in the Decision statement — it rides only in §2.2/§5.2 prose. A commitment of this reversibility cost (it can only be walked back by amending a platform principle) that constrains all future designs deserves to be stated as a first-class sub-decision in §2, where its foreclosing scope is visible, rather than as an operationalizing gloss in the collaboration section.
incoming_claim: §2.2 commits an absolute foreclosure — 'no architectural mechanism may exist whose function depends on preventing it' — restated in §5.2 as a hard constraint on all future designs ('or introduce a mechanism whose function depends on preventing it'). This is a broader, harder-to-reverse commitment than the never-preclude posture the §2 Decision statement carries (agents that 'collaborate on any route'), and it forecloses design space across every downstream SDD/DDR for the platform's life. A commitment of this reversibility cost — walkable back only by amending a platform principle — that constrains all future designs is surfaced only in §2.2/§5.2 prose and never as a first-class sub-decision in §2, where its foreclosing scope would be visible. Per the record's own governing discipline the Decision block is the contract's index; a standing constraint of this weight that the enumeration does not carry is invisible to a reader who treats the enumeration as complete.

existing_claim: §2.4's committed constraint-binding pattern rests its enforceability on 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record' (§5.3: 'The Standards plane has no ingestion path until [it] and its port land') plus the forthcoming constraint-validator design — two unauthored, not-ACCEPTED records. The record commits the pattern now while its populating substrate (Standards-plane ingestion) and its evaluation locus are both unauthored, leaving §2.4 review-enforceable-only for an open-ended interval. §5.3 states this in earned tense, which is proportionate; the timing observation is recorded here that the load-bearing new commitment depends on two unauthored downstream records at once, and the §7 cross-reference to the ingestion record carries no status marker at its §5.3 use point.
incoming_claim: §2.4's central new commitment (constraints-from-graph) rests its enforceability on 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record' (§5.3: 'The Standards plane has no ingestion path until [it] and its port land') plus the forthcoming constraint-validator design — two unauthored, not-ACCEPTED records. §5.3 states the dependency in earned tense, which is proportionate for the risk; but the §7 cross-reference entry carries only 'forthcoming' with no status marker distinguishing an unauthored record from an authored-but-unfetched one, and no resolution locus at the altitude a consequence should be declared. The record commits the pattern now while both its populating substrate and its evaluation locus are unauthored, leaving §2.4 review-enforceable-only for an open-ended interval — a timing observation on a load-bearing new commitment that depends on two unauthored downstream records at once.

existing_claim: Attacked the coordination-trajectory commitment for pulling against the ratified graph-access and write-authority posture — whether committing 'increasing direct agent-to-agent interaction' erodes the sole-owner-gateway / named-write-authority discipline by legitimizing alternate graph paths as coordination density falls. It held: §2.3 pins every route to the sole-owner gateway ('no interaction route creates a second path to the graph'), §2.7 advances 'through the upstream authority structure, never around it' and routes any authority-widening back to the owning record's amendment process, and §2.9 disclaims re-authoring ADR-002 §2.5/§2.6. The trajectory moves coordination density, not the graph-access invariant — it integrates with the platform posture rather than pulling against it.
incoming_claim: Attacked the coordination-trajectory commitment for pulling against the ratified graph-access and write-authority posture — whether committing 'increasing direct agent-to-agent interaction' erodes the sole-owner-gateway / named-write-authority discipline by legitimizing alternate graph paths as coordination density falls. It held: §2.3 pins every route to the sole-owner gateway ('no interaction route creates a second path to the graph'), §2.7 advances 'through the upstream authority structure, never around it' and routes any authority-widening back to the owning record's amendment process (naming run-lifecycle authorship beyond the AOE as the exemplar), and §2.9 disclaims re-authoring ADR-002 §2.5/§2.6. The trajectory moves coordination density, not the graph-access invariant — it integrates with the platform posture and defers to the upstream amendment process rather than carving out an upstream invariant by downstream qualifier.

existing_claim: Attacked the fold of the prior directives-propagation bridge into this record as a rejected alternative for reversibility proportionality — whether dissolving a prior integrity-machinery pattern into a §4 alternative entry, with no standalone record, under-commits a decision that deserved its own. It held: §4.3 relocates constraint authority to the graph (§2.4) rather than deleting a live mechanism, the surviving residues (correlation propagation, constraint rendering, surfaced-constraint audit) are each routed to a named owning layer, the fold is reversible by amendment here, and it homes the rejected alternative canonically. The disposition is proportionate to the decision's cost; no orphaned or under-committed position results.
incoming_claim: Attacked the fold of the prior directives-propagation bridge into this record as a rejected alternative for reversibility proportionality — whether dissolving a prior integrity-machinery pattern into a §4 alternative entry, with no standalone record, under-commits a decision that deserved its own. It held: §4.3 relocates constraint authority to the graph (§2.4) rather than deleting a live mechanism, the surviving residues (correlation propagation, constraint rendering, surfaced-constraint audit) are each routed to a named owning layer, the fold is reversible by amendment here, and it homes the rejected alternative canonically. The disposition is proportionate to the decision's cost and integrates with the platform's supersession posture; no orphaned or under-committed position results.

existing_claim: §2.6 names 'the model-access gateway (the forthcoming ai-gateway-service, §7)' as a canonical substrate-service instance ('infrastructure facade — model access, no judgment of record'), and §5.1 names 'the model-access gateway design' among the designs this record unblocks. §2.6's own governing rule immediately follows: 'This record enumerates no agent roster ... Components are named above only where existing canon already names them.' No record in the set establishes a model-access gateway / ai-gateway-service as a committed component: SDD-001 §2.2 states 'LLM access and prompt assembly — no model client exists in this service' and names no downstream gateway; ADR-001 §2.3 commits LLM-as-renderer without naming a gateway component. The record names a component upstream canon does not establish while claiming to name only canon-established ones — a scope conflict against its own stated constraint, and it commits a component-existence posture (§5.1 'unblocks the model-access gateway design') that no cited authority carries.
incoming_claim: §2.6 fixes the class of a not-yet-canonical component: 'the model-access gateway (the forthcoming ai-gateway-service, §7) is of this class (infrastructure facade — model access, no judgment of record).' §2.6's own governing rule immediately follows: 'This record enumerates no agent roster … Components are named above only where existing canon already names them.' No record in the set establishes a model-access gateway / ai-gateway-service as a committed component: SDD-001 §2.2 states 'LLM access and prompt assembly — no model client exists in this service' and names no downstream gateway; ADR-001 §2.3 commits LLM-as-renderer without naming a gateway component; §7 lists 'ai-gateway-service' only as forthcoming. §5.1 further names 'the model-access gateway design' among the designs this record unblocks. The record names a component upstream canon does not establish while claiming to name only canon-established ones — a scope conflict against its own stated constraint.

existing_claim: §2.3 asserts checkpoint gates 'anchor to checkpoints in the run and the graph, not to positions in a call graph; no routing choice can bypass a gate.' §2.2 makes direct agent-to-agent communication first-class ('Direct interaction between agents is permitted on the same terms as orchestrator-mediated interaction'), and §2.8 commits that 'opening a direct agent-to-agent route requires no ratification act; the §2.3 invariants are the entire control.' The unbypassability claim is graph-anchored: gates 'anchor to checkpoints in the run and the graph.' A direct agent-to-agent interaction that performs no graph write reaches neither the sole-owner gateway (ADR-002 §2.5, which mediates graph access) nor a graph-anchored checkpoint — so 'no routing choice can bypass a gate' is stated as a load-bearing governance guarantee but is unsubstantiated for exactly the direct-route, no-graph-write interaction the same record makes first-class. The record supplies no mechanism internal to itself, nor cites one upstream, by which a gate binds an interaction that touches no graph checkpoint.
incoming_claim: §2.3 asserts checkpoint gates 'anchor to checkpoints in the run and the graph, not to positions in a call graph; no routing choice can bypass a gate.' §2.2 makes direct agent-to-agent communication first-class ('Direct interaction between agents is permitted on the same terms as orchestrator-mediated interaction'), and §2.8 commits that 'opening a direct agent-to-agent route requires no ratification act; the §2.3 invariants are the entire control.' The unbypassability claim is graph-anchored: gates 'anchor to checkpoints in the run and the graph.' A direct agent-to-agent interaction that performs no graph write reaches neither the sole-owner gateway (ADR-002 §2.5) nor a graph-anchored checkpoint — so 'no routing choice can bypass a gate' is stated as a load-bearing governance guarantee but is unsubstantiated for exactly the direct-route, no-graph-write interaction the same record makes first-class. The record supplies no mechanism internal to itself, nor cites one upstream, by which a gate binds an interaction that touches no graph checkpoint. The §2.3 capture invariant ('reasoning produced in any interaction is captured … whoever initiated') is asserted but similarly carries no route-independent enforcement locus for a no-write direct route.

existing_claim: §2.2 states 'Agent-to-agent communication is a first-class platform capability: no platform design may preclude it, and no architectural mechanism may exist whose function depends on preventing it.' §5.2 re-states this as a hard constraint on all designs. The absolute form — 'no architectural mechanism may exist whose function depends on preventing it' — is broader than the ratified commitment it purports to operationalize: the deliberation record's ratified text is 'agent-to-agent communication is first-class and never precluded,' and the record's own self-review (Leg 3) flags '§2.2's "no mechanism whose function depends on preventing it"' as a 'conformance-shaped operationalization of the ratified never-preclude commitment' — i.e., the author acknowledges the absolute-prohibition-on-preventing-mechanisms wording is an operationalization added beyond the ratified never-preclude, not the ratified commitment itself. The stronger foreclosing form rides only in §2.2/§5.2 prose and is not surfaced as such in the §2 Decision statement (which commits agents that 'collaborate on any route').
incoming_claim: §2.2 states 'Agent-to-agent communication is a first-class platform capability: no platform design may preclude it, and no architectural mechanism may exist whose function depends on preventing it.' §5.2 re-states this as a hard constraint on all designs ('or introduce a mechanism whose function depends on preventing it'). The absolute never-a-preventing-mechanism form is a foreclosing commitment on all future designs for the platform's life, walkable back only by amending a platform principle. It is not surfaced in the §2 Decision statement, which commits agents that 'collaborate on any route' — a narrower posture. The deliberation record's ratified commitment (Item 2) is 'agent-to-agent communication is first-class and never precluded,' and the record's own Leg-3 self-review flags §2.2's 'no mechanism whose function depends on preventing it' as a 'conformance-shaped operationalization of the ratified never-preclude commitment' — i.e., the author acknowledges the absolute-prohibition wording is added beyond the ratified never-preclude. The stronger foreclosing form rides only in §2.2/§5.2 prose and is not stated as a first-class sub-decision in §2 where its foreclosing scope would be visible.

existing_claim: §2.6 routes whether the orchestrator's composition decisions constitute captured reasoning to 'the forthcoming agent-orchestration-engine service design under ADR-001 §2.4's per-stage provision.' ADR-001 §2.4 ('Lifecycle scope and design-maturity honesty') provides that per-stage reasoning-capture mechanics are 'specified in the service and data designs as they are authored' — but its object is the enterprise SDLC lifecycle stages ('from idea capture through solutioning to operational onboarding'), not an intra-run coordination component. The orchestrator is not a lifecycle stage; applying §2.4's per-stage-of-the-SDLC deferral device to an intra-run coordination component's capture question is an extension of §2.4's scope the cited text does not directly cover. The routing resolves the deferral intent, but the citation is to a provision whose stated scope is a different axis (SDLC stage, not run component).
incoming_claim: §2.6 routes whether the orchestrator's composition decisions constitute captured reasoning to 'the forthcoming agent-orchestration-engine service design under ADR-001 §2.4's per-stage provision.' ADR-001 §2.4 ('Lifecycle scope and design-maturity honesty') provides that per-stage reasoning-capture mechanics are 'specified in the service and data designs as they are authored' — but its object is the enterprise SDLC lifecycle stages ('from idea capture through solutioning to operational onboarding'), not an intra-run coordination component. The orchestrator is not a lifecycle stage; applying §2.4's per-stage-of-the-SDLC deferral device to an intra-run coordination component's capture question is an extension of §2.4's stated scope (a different axis — SDLC stage, not run component) the cited text does not directly cover.

existing_claim: §5.3 and §7 both name 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record' as the Standards-plane entry path on which §2.4's constraint-binding enforcement depends. The referent resolves within the set (ADR-008 §7: 'The forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record — owns the ingestion mechanics realizing the authoritative-source ingestion control (§2.2)'), so it is not a dangling hinge — it is a real, unauthored, load-bearing dependency. Its §7 entry carries no status marker beyond 'forthcoming' and no resolution locus (ticket-home / definition-of-done pointer) at the point the record's central new commitment (§2.4) rests its enforceability on it, whereas the corpus convention (per the author-decision-record skill's earned-tense/status-marker discipline and its use elsewhere in the set) marks such referents. §5.3 does carry the earned-tense qualifier; the §7 entry does not restate the enforcement-gating consequence at the altitude a consequence is declared.
incoming_claim: §5.3 states the load-bearing dependency in earned tense ('The Standards plane has no ingestion path until the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record and its port land'), and §2.4 states the binding pattern conditionally ('once the Standards-plane ingestion path lands (§5.3)'). The §7 entry — 'The forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record (unauthored) — the Standards-plane entry path on which §2.4's enforcement stages depend' — does carry an '(unauthored)' marker. The referent resolves within the set (ADR-008 §7 names the same record as forthcoming, owning the authoritative-source ingestion control). The residue: the §7 entry does not carry a resolution locus (ticket-home / definition-of-done pointer) at the point the record's central new commitment (§2.4) rests its enforceability on it, per the corpus body-integrity convention that forthcoming/unauthored referents carry a status marker and named resolution locus.

existing_claim: Attacked the coordination-trajectory commitment for the downstream-widening failure mode (a downstream record silently widening or carving out an upstream invariant). §2.7 does not: it advances 'through the upstream authority structure, never around it,' names run-lifecycle-authorship widening beyond the AOE (ADR-002 §2.6) as a future item requiring 'the owning record's own amendment process,' and states the record 'neither blocks nor pre-empts them.' §2.3 pins every route to the sole-owner gateway ('no interaction route creates a second path to the graph'), and §2.9 disclaims re-authoring ADR-002 §2.5/§2.6 ('Graph access and write authority are ADR-002 §2.5/§2.6's'). The trajectory changes coordination density, not the graph-access invariant — it defers to the upstream amendment process rather than rescuing an upstream invariant by downstream qualifier. The clause held against the UPSTREAM-AUTHORITY rule.
incoming_claim: Attacked the coordination-trajectory commitment for the downstream-widening failure mode — a downstream record silently widening or carving out an upstream invariant. §2.7 does not: it advances 'through the upstream authority structure, never around it,' names run-lifecycle-authorship widening beyond the AOE (ADR-002 §2.6) as a future item requiring 'the owning record's own amendment process,' and states the record 'neither blocks nor pre-empts them.' §2.3 pins every route to the sole-owner gateway ('no interaction route creates a second path to the graph'), and §2.9 disclaims re-authoring ADR-002 §2.5/§2.6 ('Graph access and write authority are ADR-002 §2.5/§2.6's'). The trajectory changes coordination density, not the graph-access invariant — it defers to the upstream amendment process rather than rescuing an upstream invariant by downstream qualifier. The attack under the UPSTREAM-AUTHORITY rule failed; the clause held.
